name: Nationwide Credit Clearing
description_short: Nationwide Credit Clearing is a Chicago and Phoenix-based credit repair firm with 25+ years of experience disputing inaccurate items on credit reports to improve consumer credit scores.
description_long:
Nationwide Credit Clearing has operated for over 25 years as a credit repair service with physical offices in Chicago and Phoenix. The company specializes in identifying and disputing inaccurate, misleading, or unverifiable information on consumer credit reports. According to their website, they deleted over 25,000 items from credit reports in the past year alone, suggesting significant volume and established dispute processes.

The company offers a complete credit repair package: obtaining your credit report, handling the entire dispute process with credit bureaus, monitoring your credit for changes, and providing 24/7 access to a customer portal (CreditProtect system) where clients can track dispute status in real time. Customers can speak with dedicated agents and receive free initial credit reports and consultations. The company advertises "inexpensive, realistic and affordable" pricing, though specific fee structures are not detailed on the website.

Nationwide Credit Clearing differentiates itself through longevity (25 years in operation), claimed expertise from testing and refining their dispute methodology over decades, and dual-office presence allowing service to multiple states. They market heavily on customer testimonials showing credit score improvements into the 700s and successful mortgage/auto loan approvals after service. The company emphasizes privacy and explicitly states they do not sell customer information to third parties.

Key limitations include lack of transparency about specific pricing and service timelines on the public website. No information is provided about dispute success rates, average credit score improvements, or how their approach differs from competitors. The testimonials, while positive, are limited in number (14 reviews shown) and lack detailed metrics. There is no clear disclosure of what "25,000 items deleted" means—whether these are individual accounts or line items—or the percentage success rate of disputes.
pros: 25+ years of operating experience in credit repair industry
Deleted over 25,000 items from credit reports in the past year
Operates physical offices in two states (Chicago and Phoenix)
Free credit report and consultation with no upfront obligation
24/7 access to CreditProtect portal to monitor dispute status and progress
Explicitly commits to privacy with guarantee not to sell customer information
Customer testimonials show real results (credit scores into 700s, mortgage/auto approvals)
cons: Specific pricing and fee structure not disclosed on website—requires contact for quote
No published dispute success rates or average credit score improvement metrics
Limited transparency about typical dispute timelines (one testimonial mentions 4 months)
Only 14 customer reviews published, which is relatively small sample size for 25-year-old company
No detailed explanation of what constitutes the '25,000 items deleted' claim or verification methodology
best_for: Consumers in Illinois or Arizona with inaccurate negative items on credit reports
People preparing to apply for mortgages or auto loans within 3-6 months
Individuals who prefer working with established, brick-and-mortar credit repair firms over online-only services
diagnosis: Nationwide Credit Clearing is best suited for consumers in Illinois or Arizona with verifiable errors on their credit reports who want hands-off dispute management from an established firm. The main caveat is that detailed pricing, dispute success rates, and timeline guarantees are unavailable on their website—interested consumers must call (773) 862-7700 to evaluate whether their services justify the cost versus DIY disputes or free credit counseling.
services: Free credit report retrieval and analysis
Credit dispute filing and management with bureaus
Ongoing credit monitoring and change alerts
24/7 customer portal access (CreditProtect system) for status tracking
Removal of inaccurate, misleading, or unverifiable credit report items
Dedicated agent assignment and customer support
